key_insight: III-nitride HEMTs can operate at elevated temperatures, but device performance and stability depend strongly on barrier/channel design, substrate choice, and passivation.
engineering_relevance: The paper maps practical design knobs for building more reliable high-temperature electronics for power, RF, aerospace, and space systems.
method: Analyze high-temperature effects at the material, device, and circuit levels
Compare barrier and channel engineering, substrate selection, and passivation strategies
Assess thermal stability across logic, radiofrequency, and power applications
evidence_signal: The abstract describes a comparative analysis of high-temperature operation and performance evolution rather than reporting a single experimental dataset.
watch_item: Thermal limits remain application-specific, so results must be translated carefully to real operating conditions and long-duration reliability.
paper_title: High-temperature operation of III-nitride high-electron-mobility transistors
